target.title: fix(doctor): prevent non-interactive --fix from auto-restarting gateway
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `doctor --fix --non-interactive` auto-approves gateway service start/restart lifecycle prompts via `shouldAutoApproveDoctorFix`, which can trigger `service.restart()` and SIGTERM a running gateway after a transient health probe timeout.
- Solution: Mark gateway "Start" and "Restart" service lifecycle prompts with `requiresInteractiveConfirmation: true` so `confirmRuntimeRepair` skips them in non-interactive mode instead of auto-approving.
- What changed: `src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.ts` — added `requiresInteractiveConfirmation: true` to both the start and restart prompt calls in `maybeRepairGatewayDaemon`.
- What did NOT change: Interactive doctor behavior, install prompts, update-repair path, restart-handoff skip path, launchd/LaunchAgent handling.

Fixes #78217

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `doctor --fix --non-interactive` auto-restarting a running gateway service after transient health probe timeout
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.0 (darwin), Node 24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.8, worktree SHA 15fac61
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # Start gateway (installed as LaunchAgent)
  openclaw gateway start
  # Verify gateway is running
  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
  # {"ok":true,"status":"live"}

  # Run doctor --fix --non-interactive with patched build
  pnpm openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive

  # Verify gateway survived and was NOT restarted
  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
  # {"ok":true,"status":"live"} — same PID before and after

  # Regression tests
  npx vitest run src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  └  Doctor complete.

  === AFTER: gateway still running ===
  {"ok":true,"status":"live"}
  Gateway PID after: 64880

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  25 passed (25)
  ```
  Doctor completed without restarting the gateway (PID unchanged: 64880). The two new regression tests `skips running service restart during non-interactive repairs` and `skips stopped service start during non-interactive repairs` both pass: `service.restart()` is never called during non-interactive repair.
- **Observed result after fix:** `doctor --fix --non-interactive` completes all safe-live fixes (session snapshots, legacy state, skills, config, plugin registry) and skips gateway service lifecycle prompts in non-interactive mode. The running gateway is not restarted. The `requiresInteractiveConfirmation: true` flag causes `confirmRuntimeRepair` to return `false` when the prompter lacks interactive capability.
- **What was not tested:** Live systemd-supervised gateway restart on Linux (identical code path; `confirmRuntimeRepair` behavior is platform-agnostic)

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Non-interactive repair skips restart for running services; non-interactive repair skips start for stopped services
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The two new tests use the same `runNonInteractiveRepair` helper already covering the update-repair path, asserting `service.restart()` is never called — the exact failure mode reported in the issue.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `confirmDoctorServiceRepair` → `confirmRuntimeRepair` calls `shouldAutoApproveDoctorFix(repairMode, { blockDuringUpdate: true })` which returns `true` for `--fix --non-interactive` when `requiresInteractiveConfirmation` is NOT set. The "Start gateway service now?" and "Restart gateway service now?" prompts lacked this flag, so they were auto-approved.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Service lifecycle prompts (start, restart) that can terminate a running gateway had no `requiresInteractiveConfirmation` guard, unlike the install prompt which already requires interactive confirmation.
